movements.name: Ab Mat Sit Up
movements.description: Sit-up performed with an Ab Mat under the lower back to allow a deeper hip extension at the bottom of each rep, feet anchored or held by a partner.

movements.name: Air Squat
movements.description: A bodyweight squat to at least hip-crease-below-knee depth and back to standing, with no external load — the foundational squat pattern everything else builds on.

movements.name: Alternating Dumbbell Power Snatch
movements.description: Alternating dumbbell snatch caught without a full squat, just a partial dip-and-catch.

movements.name: Alternating Dumbbell Snatch
movements.description: Dumbbell snatch performed for reps, switching arms either every rep or on a set schedule per the workout.

movements.name: American Kettlebell Swing
movements.description: Kettlebell swing driven all the way to an overhead lockout at the top of each rep, rather than stopping at chest height.

movements.name: Assault Bike
movements.description: A prescribed calorie count or distance on an air-resistance fan bike (Assault Bike/AirBike), which uses both arms and legs.

movements.name: Atlas Stone Over Shoulder
movements.description: Lifting a round, weighted atlas stone from the ground and placing it over one shoulder in a single motion.

movements.name: Back Extension
movements.description: On a GHD or hyperextension bench, hinging the torso down and raising it back up to extend the lower back and glutes.

movements.name: Back Rack Lunge
movements.description: Barbell racked on the back as in a back squat while stepping into a lunge, alternating legs — combines squat-pattern loading with single-leg stability.

movements.name: Back Squat
movements.description: A barbell held across the upper back (low- or high-bar) while squatting to at least hip-crease-below-knee depth, then standing back up — the foundational lower-body strength lift.

movements.name: Ball Slam
movements.description: Raising a medicine ball overhead and slamming it forcefully into the ground, then picking it back up for the next rep.

movements.name: Bar Dip
movements.description: Dip performed on parallel bars, lowering the shoulders below the elbows and pressing back to full lockout.

movements.name: Bar Facing Burpee
movements.description: Burpee performed facing a barbell (or other low bar), jumping over it with both feet together after standing up from each rep.

movements.name: Bar Muscle Up
movements.description: Muscle-up performed on a straight pull-up bar rather than gymnastics rings.

movements.name: Barbell Overhead Lunge
movements.description: Barbell locked out overhead while lunging forward or walking — combines overhead stability with a single-leg squat pattern.

movements.name: Barbell Step Up
movements.description: Stepping up onto a box with a loaded barbell on the back or in the front rack, driving through the lead leg to full hip extension at the top.

movements.name: Bear Crawl
movements.description: Crawling forward on hands and feet with the knees hovering just off the ground, opposite hand and foot moving together.

movements.name: Bench Press
movements.description: Barbell lowered to the chest while lying on a bench and pressed back to full lockout — the classic horizontal pressing strength lift.

movements.name: Bent Over Row
movements.description: Torso hinged forward and near-parallel to the floor, pulling a barbell to the lower ribs/stomach and lowering under control — targets the mid-back and lats.

movements.name: Bike
movements.description: Riding a prescribed distance or calorie count on a stationary bike.

movements.name: Bike Erg
movements.description: A prescribed distance or calorie count on a Concept2 BikeErg, a standard-geometry stationary bike ergometer.

movements.name: Bodyweight Clean And Jerk
movements.description: A clean-and-jerk loaded relative to the athlete's own bodyweight rather than a fixed load, so the percentage — not the number on the bar — is the prescription.

movements.name: Box Jump
movements.description: Jumping with both feet from the ground onto a raised box, standing to full hip extension at the top, then stepping or jumping back down.

movements.name: Box Jump Over
movements.description: Jumping with both feet onto a box and immediately continuing over the top to land on the other side, rather than standing on top of it.

movements.name: Box Squat
movements.description: Back squat performed to a box or bench, briefly sitting back to touch it before standing — reinforces hip-driven depth and a controlled bottom position.

movements.name: Box Step Up
movements.description: Stepping up onto a box one foot at a time (no jump), driving through the lead leg to full hip extension at the top.

movements.name: Broad Jump
movements.description: A standing jump for maximum forward distance, taking off and landing on both feet.

movements.name: Burpee
movements.description: From standing, dropping to the floor with the chest touching down, then pushing back up and jumping with a clap overhead — the classic full-body conditioning movement.

movements.name: Burpee Bar Muscle Up
movements.description: A standard burpee immediately followed by one bar muscle-up before the next rep begins.

movements.name: Burpee Box Jump
movements.description: Burpee immediately followed by jumping onto a box and standing to full hip extension at the top.

movements.name: Burpee Box Jump Over
movements.description: Burpee immediately followed by jumping onto and completely over a box, landing on the other side.

movements.name: Burpee Broad Jump
movements.description: Burpee immediately followed by a maximal-distance broad jump forward instead of a vertical jump.

movements.name: Burpee Muscle Up
movements.description: A standard burpee immediately followed by one muscle-up before the next rep begins.

movements.name: Burpee Over Dumbbell
movements.description: Burpee performed straddling or beside a dumbbell, jumping over it after standing up from each rep.

movements.name: Burpee Over The Bar
movements.description: Burpee performed beside a barbell, jumping or stepping laterally over it after standing up from each rep.

movements.name: Burpee Pull Up
movements.description: A standard burpee immediately followed by one pull-up before the next rep begins.

movements.name: Butterfly Pull Up
movements.description: A continuous, circular kipping pull-up where the hips trace a loop rather than a front-to-back swing — the fastest competitive pull-up style once the timing is mastered.

movements.name: Chest To Bar Pull Up
movements.description: Pull-up (strict or kipping) where the chest, not just the chin, must reach the bar at the top of every rep — a longer range of motion than a standard pull-up.

movements.name: Chin Up
movements.description: Pull-up performed with an underhand (supinated) grip rather than overhand — the biceps-forward grip variation.

movements.name: Clean
movements.description: Barbell pulled from the floor and received in a front-rack squat in one continuous motion — the catch-all term for the squat clean unless a variant is specified.

movements.name: Clean And Jerk
movements.description: A clean immediately followed by a jerk to lock the bar out overhead — the classic two-lift combination used to move the heaviest loads from the ground to overhead.

movements.name: Clean Pull
movements.description: The pulling portion of a clean (floor to full extension) performed without racking the bar — used to build pulling strength and speed without the catch.

movements.name: Cossack Squat
movements.description: A wide-stance side-to-side squat, shifting weight fully onto one bent leg while the other stays straight, then shifting to the other side.

movements.name: Crossover Single Under
movements.description: Single-under jump rope where the arms cross in front of the body as the rope passes underfoot.

movements.name: D Ball Clean
movements.description: A D-ball (a round, textured medicine-ball-style implement) pulled from the floor and received at the chest/shoulder, similar to a sandbag or medicine-ball clean.

movements.name: D Ball Over Shoulder
movements.description: Lifting a D-ball from the ground and placing it over one shoulder in a single motion.

movements.name: Deadlift
movements.description: Barbell lifted from the floor to full hip and knee extension with a flat back and the bar close to the shins — the fundamental hip-hinge strength movement.

movements.name: Deficit Deadlift
movements.description: Deadlift performed standing on a raised platform, increasing the range of motion off the floor — demands extra hip and ankle mobility off the bottom.

movements.name: Deficit Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up with the hands elevated on parallettes or plates, increasing the range of motion by lowering the head below hand height.

movements.name: Devils Press
movements.description: A burpee with a dumbbell in each hand — chest to the floor beside the dumbbells, then standing and driving straight into a dumbbell snatch to overhead in one motion, both arms together.

movements.name: Double Kettlebell Farmers Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance holding a kettlebell in each hand at the sides.

movements.name: Double Kettlebell Front Rack Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance holding a kettlebell in each hand racked at the shoulders.

movements.name: Double Kettlebell Front Rack Lunge
movements.description: Lunge holding a kettlebell in each hand racked at the shoulders, alternating legs.

movements.name: Double Kettlebell Overhead Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance with a kettlebell locked out overhead in each hand.

movements.name: Double Under
movements.description: Jump rope pass where the rope passes under the feet twice per single jump — a faster wrist-driven turn than a basic single jump.

movements.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
movements.description: Bench press performed with a dumbbell in each hand instead of a barbell, allowing a greater range of motion and independent arm paths.

movements.name: Dumbbell Box Step Up
movements.description: Stepping up onto a box holding a dumbbell in each hand (or at the shoulders), driving through the lead leg to full hip extension at the top.

movements.name: Dumbbell Burpee Deadlift
movements.description: A burpee performed with a pair of dumbbells on the floor — chest to the ground between the dumbbells, then standing up and deadlifting them before the next rep.

movements.name: Dumbbell Carry
movements.description: Carrying one or two dumbbells over a set distance, position (front rack, overhead, or at the sides) specified by the workout.

movements.name: Dumbbell Clean
movements.description: A dumbbell (or pair) pulled from the floor and received in the front rack, typically with a slight squat — the dumbbell analog of the barbell clean.

movements.name: Dumbbell Clean And Jerk
movements.description: A dumbbell clean immediately followed by a jerk to lock the dumbbell(s) out overhead.

movements.name: Dumbbell Deadlift
movements.description: Deadlift performed holding a dumbbell in each hand (or one between the feet) instead of a barbell.

movements.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
movements.description: Dumbbell bench press performed lying on the floor, stopping the descent when the upper arms touch the ground.

movements.name: Dumbbell Front Rack Lunge
movements.description: Lunge holding dumbbell(s) in the front rack at the shoulders rather than at the sides, adding a core/upper-back demand to the leg work.

movements.name: Dumbbell Front Rack Squat
movements.description: Squat holding a dumbbell (or pair) in the front rack position at the shoulders throughout the descent and stand.

movements.name: Dumbbell Front Squat
movements.description: Front squat holding a dumbbell at each shoulder (or one at the chest) instead of a barbell.

movements.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
movements.description: Squat holding a single dumbbell vertically against the chest with both hands cupping the top end — an accessible, upright-torso squat variation.

movements.name: Dumbbell Hang Clean And Jerk
movements.description: Dumbbell clean initiated from the hang, followed by a jerk to lock it out overhead.

movements.name: Dumbbell Hang Clean To Overhead
movements.description: Dumbbell hang clean immediately followed by any method of getting it overhead (press, push press, or jerk), per the athlete's choice.

movements.name: Dumbbell Hang Power Clean
movements.description: Dumbbell hang clean caught above parallel with only a partial dip.

movements.name: Dumbbell Hang Split Snatch
movements.description: Dumbbell snatch initiated from the hang and received in a front-to-back split stance overhead.

movements.name: Dumbbell Hang Squat Clean
movements.description: Dumbbell squat clean initiated from a hang position at or above the knee rather than pulling from the floor.

movements.name: Dumbbell Overhead Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance with one or two dumbbells locked out overhead, arms fully extended throughout.

movements.name: Dumbbell Overhead Lunge
movements.description: Lunge with one or two dumbbells locked out overhead throughout — a stability-heavy single-leg movement.

movements.name: Dumbbell Overhead Squat
movements.description: Squat with one or two dumbbells locked out overhead throughout the full range of motion — demands significant shoulder stability.

movements.name: Dumbbell Power Clean
movements.description: Dumbbell clean caught above parallel with only a partial dip rather than a full-depth squat.

movements.name: Dumbbell Power Snatch
movements.description: Dumbbell snatch caught with a partial dip rather than a full squat receive — usually interchangeable with dumbbell-snatch, naming the catch depth explicitly.

movements.name: Dumbbell Push Jerk
movements.description: Dumbbell driven overhead with a dip-drive and a quarter-squat receive — the dumbbell version of the push jerk.

movements.name: Dumbbell Push Press
movements.description: Dumbbell pressed overhead using a short dip-and-drive from the legs — the dumbbell version of the barbell push press.

movements.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
movements.description: Romanian deadlift performed holding dumbbells at the sides instead of a barbell in front.

movements.name: Dumbbell Row
movements.description: Single-arm dumbbell pulled from a hang to the hip/ribs, typically bracing on a bench or in a bent-over stance — targets the lat and mid-back.

movements.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
movements.description: Dumbbell pressed from the shoulder to locked-out overhead with no leg drive — a strict, single- or double-arm overhead press.

movements.name: Dumbbell Shoulder To Overhead
movements.description: Any method of moving a dumbbell from the shoulder to locked-out overhead by the athlete's choice — press, push press, or push jerk.

movements.name: Dumbbell Snatch
movements.description: A single dumbbell pulled from the floor to locked-out overhead in one continuous motion, typically alternating arms — the dumbbell analog of the barbell snatch.

movements.name: Dumbbell Split Clean
movements.description: Dumbbell clean received in a front-to-back split stance rather than a squat, similar in spirit to a split jerk's receiving position.

movements.name: Dumbbell Squat Clean
movements.description: Dumbbell clean received in a full-depth front squat before standing.

movements.name: Dumbbell Squat Clean Thruster
movements.description: A dumbbell squat clean received in the bottom of the squat and driven straight up into an overhead press without standing up in between — one continuous rep.

movements.name: Dumbbell Strict Press
movements.description: Dumbbell pressed overhead using shoulder strength alone, no hip or leg drive, matching the strictness standard of a barbell strict press.

movements.name: Dumbbell Thruster
movements.description: Front squat with a dumbbell (or two) at the shoulders driven directly into an overhead press as the athlete stands — the dumbbell version of the barbell thruster.

movements.name: Dumbbell Waiters Walk
movements.description: Walking a set distance with a single dumbbell held overhead on a flat palm, arm fully locked out — a unilateral overhead-stability carry.

movements.name: Dumbbell Walking Lunge
movements.description: Walking lunge holding a dumbbell in each hand (or one at the shoulder), alternating legs with each step.

movements.name: Echo Bike
movements.description: A prescribed calorie count or distance on an Echo Bike, a fan bike similar in use to an Assault Bike.

movements.name: Farmers Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance holding a heavy dumbbell (or other implement) in each hand at the sides — a grip- and core-bracing loaded carry.

movements.name: Floor Press
movements.description: Bench press performed lying on the floor instead of a bench, so the range of motion stops when the upper arms touch the ground — reduces shoulder strain and emphasizes lockout strength.

movements.name: Flutter Kick
movements.description: Lying on the back with legs extended and slightly raised, kicking the legs in a small, rapid alternating up-down motion.

movements.name: Forward Roll
movements.description: A tucked forward somersault along the ground, rolling from the shoulders through to standing.

movements.name: Freestanding Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up performed balancing in the middle of the floor with no wall for support — demands full handstand balance in addition to the press.

movements.name: Front Rack Lunge
movements.description: Barbell held in the front rack (as in a front squat) while lunging — the upright torso the front rack demands makes this more quad- and core-dominant than a back-rack lunge.

movements.name: Front Squat
movements.description: Barbell racked across the front deltoids and collarbones with elbows high, squatting to full depth — builds quad and core strength and carries directly into the clean.

movements.name: Ghd Hip Extension
movements.description: Hip-focused variation of the back extension on a GHD, emphasizing glute drive to extend the hips rather than lower-back rounding/extension.

movements.name: Ghd Sit Up
movements.description: Sit-up performed on a Glute-Ham Developer, hips anchored so the torso can hinge well past parallel behind the hips before curling all the way forward to touch the toes — a much larger range of motion than a floor sit-up.

movements.name: Goblet Squat
movements.description: Squat holding a single kettlebell (or dumbbell) vertically against the chest with both hands cupping the horns/top — an accessible, upright-torso squat.

movements.name: Good Morning
movements.description: Barbell on the back, hinging forward at the hips with a flat back and soft knees until the torso nears parallel to the floor, then returning upright — targets the posterior chain.

movements.name: Ground To Overhead
movements.description: Any barbell movement taking the bar from the floor to locked out overhead by whatever method the athlete chooses — clean-and-jerk, clean-and-press, or a muscle clean into a press.

movements.name: Hand Release Push Up
movements.description: Push-up where the hands lift completely off the floor at the bottom of each rep before pressing back up — ensures full chest-to-floor depth on every rep.

movements.name: Handstand
movements.description: Balancing inverted on the hands, whether freestanding or against a wall, holding the body in a straight vertical line.

movements.name: Handstand Hold
movements.description: Holding a handstand position for time, freestanding or wall-supported per the workout's standard.

movements.name: Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Inverted against a wall for balance, lowering the head to the floor (or a target) and pressing back to a locked-out handstand — the vertical pressing bodyweight movement.

movements.name: Handstand Walk
movements.description: Walking on the hands while inverted in a handstand, covering a set distance.

movements.name: Hang Clean
movements.description: Clean initiated from a 'hang' position (bar at or above the knee, not the floor) rather than pulling from the ground — removes the initial floor pull, emphasizing the second pull and catch.

movements.name: Hang Power Clean
movements.description: Hang clean caught above parallel in a partial-depth squat rather than full depth.

movements.name: Hang Power Snatch
movements.description: Hang snatch caught above parallel rather than in a full overhead squat.

movements.name: Hang Snatch
movements.description: Snatch initiated from the hang position rather than the floor, emphasizing the second pull and the catch.

movements.name: Hang Squat Clean
movements.description: Clean started from the hang position and received in a full-depth front squat.

movements.name: Hanging Hip Touch
movements.description: Hanging from a bar or rings, raising the legs to touch the hips to the bar/rings — an advanced core movement between knees-to-elbows and toes-to-bar in difficulty.

movements.name: Hanging Knee Raise
movements.description: Hanging from a bar, raising the knees toward the chest under control with little to no kip — an ab/hip-flexor strength movement.

movements.name: High Knees
movements.description: Running in place (or traveling) driving the knees up toward hip height with each step, at a fast cadence.

movements.name: Hip Extension
movements.description: General term for extending the hips from a flexed position back to neutral/extended against resistance or bodyweight, as prescribed on a GHD or hyperextension bench.

movements.name: Hollow Hold
movements.description: Holding the hollow body position (lower back flat to the floor, shoulders and legs raised) motionless for time.

movements.name: Hollow Rock
movements.description: Holding a hollow body position (lower back pressed to the floor, arms and legs extended and slightly raised) while rocking gently front to back — a core-control and rhythm drill.

movements.name: Husafell Carry
movements.description: Carrying a Husafell stone (an odd-shaped, bear-hugged concrete/rubber implement) a set distance against the chest.

movements.name: Inchworm
movements.description: From standing, folding forward to place the hands on the floor, walking the hands out to a plank, then walking the feet up to the hands to return to standing.

movements.name: Jerk
movements.description: Barbell driven from the front rack to locked-out overhead using a dip-drive and a foot-split (or push jerk) receiving position — the second half of the clean-and-jerk.

movements.name: Jerry Bag Carry
movements.description: Carrying a loaded jerry-can-style bag (handles on the sides) a set distance, typically at the sides or chest.

movements.name: Jumping Jack
movements.description: Jumping to spread the feet wide while raising the arms overhead, then jumping back to the start position — a basic warm-up/conditioning movement.

movements.name: Jumping Lunge
movements.description: Lunge where the athlete jumps to switch the front/back leg mid-air between reps, rather than stepping.

movements.name: Jumping Pull Up
movements.description: Pull-up assisted by a small jump off the ground to help the chin clear the bar — a scaled version of a full pull-up for athletes still building pulling strength.

movements.name: Kayak
movements.description: Paddling a kayak a prescribed distance.

movements.name: Kettlebell Clean
movements.description: Kettlebell pulled from between the legs (or the floor) and received in the front rack against the forearm, absorbing it softly to avoid banging the wrist.

movements.name: Kettlebell Clean And Jerk
movements.description: A kettlebell clean immediately followed by driving it overhead with a jerk-style dip and drive.

movements.name: Kettlebell Deadlift
movements.description: Deadlift performed holding a kettlebell (often between the feet or held with both hands) instead of a barbell.

movements.name: Kettlebell Front Rack Lunge
movements.description: Lunge holding one or two kettlebells in the front rack against the shoulders.

movements.name: Kettlebell Halo
movements.description: Circling a kettlebell around the head, close to the skull, reversing direction each rep or set — a shoulder-mobility and control movement, not a strength lift.

movements.name: Kettlebell Overhead Lunge
movements.description: Lunge with a kettlebell locked out overhead throughout — demands shoulder stability on top of the single-leg squat pattern.

movements.name: Kettlebell Press
movements.description: Kettlebell pressed from the shoulder/rack position to locked-out overhead using shoulder strength alone.

movements.name: Kettlebell Push Press
movements.description: Kettlebell pressed overhead using a short dip-and-drive from the legs rather than a strict press.

movements.name: Kettlebell Snatch
movements.description: A single kettlebell pulled from between the legs to locked-out overhead in one continuous motion, the bell rolling smoothly around the wrist rather than banging into the forearm.

movements.name: Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pull
movements.description: Kettlebell deadlifted from a sumo stance and pulled explosively upward to about chin height, elbows leading.

movements.name: Kettlebell Swing
movements.description: A kettlebell hiked back between the legs and driven forward by the hips to about chest or eye height — a hip-hinge power movement, not a shoulder lift.

movements.name: Kettlebell Thruster
movements.description: Front squat holding a kettlebell in the front rack (or goblet position), driven directly into an overhead press as the athlete stands.

movements.name: Kettlebell Windmill
movements.description: Standing with a kettlebell locked out overhead, hinging sideways at the hips to touch (or reach toward) the opposite foot while keeping the arm vertical — an overhead-stability and hip-mobility drill.

movements.name: Kipping Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up using a kip (a hip/knee drive against the wall) to help press back up out of the bottom, similar in spirit to a kipping pull-up.

movements.name: Kipping Pull Up
movements.description: Pull-up using a rhythmic hip/leg swing (the 'kip') to generate momentum, getting the chin over the bar faster and with less pure pulling strength than a strict rep.

movements.name: Knees To Elbows
movements.description: Hanging from a bar, bringing both knees up to touch the elbows and lowering back — a shorter-range regression of toes-to-bar.

movements.name: L Pull Up
movements.description: Strict pull-up performed with the legs held straight out in front at a 90-degree angle (an L-sit position) for the entire rep — adds a core-and-hip-flexor demand to the pull.

movements.name: L Sit
movements.description: Supporting the body on the hands (on the floor, a box, or rings) with the legs held straight out in front at a 90-degree angle, held for time.

movements.name: Lateral Burpee
movements.description: Burpee where the jump/stand-up finishes with a lateral jump over a line or low object, changing sides each rep.

movements.name: Legless Rope Climb
movements.description: Rope climb performed using the arms and grip alone, with no assistance from the legs or feet on the rope.

movements.name: Log Carry
movements.description: Carrying a loaded wooden or synthetic log a set distance, typically bear-hugged against the chest.

movements.name: Log Clean And Jerk
movements.description: A loaded log pulled from the floor to the chest/shoulders and then driven overhead to a lockout, similar in structure to a barbell clean-and-jerk but with an axle-style implement.

movements.name: Log Press
movements.description: Pressing a loaded log from the shoulders/chest to locked-out overhead, typically without a squat clean to get it there first.

movements.name: Medicine Ball Box Step Over
movements.description: Stepping up and completely over a box while holding a medicine ball at the chest, landing on the other side.

movements.name: Medicine Ball Clean
movements.description: A medicine ball pulled from the floor and received at the shoulder/chest in a partial squat, similar in spirit to a barbell clean but with a ball instead of a bar.

movements.name: Medicine Ball Sit Up
movements.description: Sit-up performed holding a medicine ball at the chest (or throwing it to a partner at the top), adding load to the standard sit-up.

movements.name: Medicine Ball Throw
movements.description: Throwing a medicine ball for maximum distance or height, or at a partner/target, per the workout's standard.

movements.name: Mountain Climber
movements.description: From a plank position, rapidly driving alternating knees toward the chest while the hands stay planted.

movements.name: Muscle Clean
movements.description: Clean received standing tall with no dip under the bar — pulled high enough to rack it without a re-bend of the knees, emphasizing pull strength over speed-under-the-bar.

movements.name: Muscle Snatch
movements.description: Snatch pulled and pressed out overhead with no re-bend of the knees to catch it — an overhead-strength and technique builder, not a max-load lift.

movements.name: Muscle Up
movements.description: A pull-up that continues past the bar/rings into a dip lockout above them in one continuous motion — combines a pull and a press into a single rep.

movements.name: Negative Pull Up
movements.description: Starting at the top of a pull-up (chin over the bar) and lowering as slowly as possible to a full hang — builds pulling strength by isolating the eccentric portion.

movements.name: Obstacle Pirouette
movements.description: A rotational transition move over a raised obstacle or bar, pivoting the body around a single point of contact to clear it — a Games-specific obstacle-course skill.

movements.name: Overhead Squat
movements.description: Barbell locked out overhead in a wide snatch grip while squatting to full depth — demands shoulder stability, ankle/hip mobility, and a tight overhead position throughout.

movements.name: Overhead Walk
movements.description: Walking a set distance with a loaded barbell locked out overhead — a loaded carry that tests overhead stability and core bracing under fatigue.

movements.name: Overhead Walking Lunge
movements.description: Walking lunge performed with the arms locked out overhead throughout, adding a stability demand to the traveling lunge.

movements.name: Paddleboard
movements.description: Paddling a standup paddleboard a prescribed distance.

movements.name: Parallel Bar Traverse
movements.description: Moving hand-over-hand along a row of parallel bars while supporting the body weight on straight arms, without the feet touching the ground.

movements.name: Parallette Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up performed with hands on parallettes, adding a deficit and requiring wrist/shoulder stability on the narrow bars.

movements.name: Pegboard
movements.description: Climbing a vertical board studded with holes by moving a pair of wooden pegs upward hand over hand, to a marked height and back down.

movements.name: Pendlay Row
movements.description: Bent-over row performed with the bar returning to a dead stop on the floor between every rep, torso held parallel throughout — a stricter, more explosive row variant.

movements.name: Pistol
movements.description: A single-leg squat to full depth with the non-working leg held straight out in front, then standing back up on the one leg — demands significant single-leg strength and balance.

movements.name: Plank
movements.description: Holding the body in a straight line supported on the forearms (or hands) and toes, core braced, for time.

movements.name: Plate Carry
movements.description: Carrying one or two weight plates a set distance, typically pinched at the sides or held at the chest.

movements.name: Plate Ground To Overhead
movements.description: Moving a weight plate from the floor to locked-out overhead by any method the athlete chooses.

movements.name: Power Clean
movements.description: Barbell pulled from the floor and caught in the front rack above parallel (a partial-depth squat) — demands more raw pulling power since there's less assistance from the squat.

movements.name: Power Snatch
movements.description: Snatch caught overhead above parallel in a partial-depth squat rather than a full overhead squat.

movements.name: Pull Over
movements.description: Starting in a support position on a bar, rotating the body up and over the bar in a continuous swing to return to support facing the other way.

movements.name: Pull Up
movements.description: Hanging from a bar with an overhand grip, pulling the chin over the bar and lowering back to a full hang — the baseline pulling bodyweight movement, strict or kipping depending on the workout.

movements.name: Push Jerk
movements.description: Jerk received with both feet moving straight out to a quarter-squat rather than splitting front-to-back.

movements.name: Push Press
movements.description: Barbell pressed overhead using a short leg dip-and-drive for momentum, rather than a strict press from a static stand.

movements.name: Push Up
movements.description: From a plank position, lowering the chest to the floor and pressing back to full arm extension — the baseline horizontal pressing bodyweight movement.

movements.name: Renegade Row
movements.description: From a push-up (plank) position with a hand on each dumbbell, rowing one dumbbell to the hip while balancing on the other — combines a core-stability plank with a rowing pull.

movements.name: Reverse Lunge
movements.description: Lunge stepping backward instead of forward, then returning to standing before the next rep.

movements.name: Ring Dip
movements.description: Dip performed supporting the body on gymnastics rings instead of parallel bars, adding significant stability demand as the rings want to swing and rotate.

movements.name: Ring Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up performed with the hands on gymnastics rings instead of the floor, adding significant instability to the press.

movements.name: Ring Lower
movements.description: Lowering the body from a support or muscle-up position on the rings down to a hang (or through the dip) under control, isolating the eccentric portion of a ring movement.

movements.name: Ring Muscle Up
movements.description: Muscle-up performed on gymnastics rings, which allow the hands to rotate through the transition — generally considered more technical than the bar version.

movements.name: Ring Push Up
movements.description: Push-up performed with hands on gymnastics rings instead of the floor, adding an instability demand to the standard push-up.

movements.name: Ring Row
movements.description: Body held at an angle underneath a set of rings (or a bar), pulling the chest to the rings and lowering back — a horizontal, scalable pulling movement.

movements.name: Romanian Deadlift
movements.description: Barbell lowered from the hips (not the floor) with knees only slightly bent, pushing the hips back until a hamstring stretch is felt, then driving back to standing.

movements.name: Rope Climb
movements.description: Climbing a suspended rope hand-over-hand (with or without the feet/legs for assistance) to a marked height, then descending under control.

movements.name: Row
movements.description: Rowing a prescribed distance or calorie count on a rowing ergometer (Concept2 or similar).

movements.name: Run
movements.description: Running a prescribed distance, typically outdoors or on a track, at whatever pace the workout's format calls for.

movements.name: Run Backward
movements.description: Running a prescribed distance moving backward instead of forward.

movements.name: Russian Kettlebell Swing
movements.description: Kettlebell swing driven only to approximately chest/eye height, arms staying roughly parallel to the floor at the top.

movements.name: Sandbag Carry
movements.description: Carrying a loaded sandbag a set distance, held however the workout specifies (bear hug, shoulder, or overhead).

movements.name: Sandbag Clean
movements.description: A sandbag pulled from the floor and received at the shoulder or chest in one motion, similar to a barbell/dumbbell clean but with an unwieldy, shifting load.

movements.name: Sandbag Ground To Shoulder
movements.description: Any method of moving a sandbag from the floor to one shoulder — a clean, a lap-and-heave, or a squat-and-press, athlete's choice unless specified.

movements.name: Sandbag Over Log
movements.description: Lifting a loaded sandbag up and over a log or barrier obstacle.

movements.name: Sandbag Squat
movements.description: Squat holding a sandbag at the chest, on the back, or on the shoulders per the workout's prescription.

movements.name: Shoulder To Overhead
movements.description: Any method of moving a loaded barbell from the front rack to locked-out overhead by the athlete's choice — strict press, push press, or jerk.

movements.name: Shuttle Run
movements.description: Running back and forth between two fixed points a set number of times, rather than a single straight-line distance.

movements.name: Shuttle Sprint
movements.description: Sprinting to a marked line and back (or between two lines repeatedly) rather than a single straight-line distance.

movements.name: Single Arm Dumbbell Thruster
movements.description: Thruster performed holding one dumbbell in a single-arm front rack, alternating or fixed to one side per the workout's prescription — adds an anti-rotation core demand.

movements.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Swing
movements.description: Kettlebell swing performed with one arm, often switching hands mid-set — adds an anti-rotation core demand to the standard two-handed swing.

movements.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Thruster
movements.description: Kettlebell thruster performed holding the bell in one arm's front rack, adding an anti-rotation core demand.

movements.name: Single Under
movements.description: A standard jump rope pass, one rotation of the rope per jump.

movements.name: Sit Up
movements.description: Lying on the back with knees bent and feet anchored, curling the torso up to touch the chest to the knees (or per the workout's standard) and lowering back down.

movements.name: Ski Erg
movements.description: A prescribed distance or calorie count on a SkiErg machine, mimicking a double-pole cross-country ski motion.

movements.name: Sled Pull
movements.description: Pulling a loaded sled a set distance, either walking backward facing it or via a rope while facing away.

movements.name: Sled Push
movements.description: Pushing a loaded sled across a set distance, driving through the legs with arms extended and braced against the sled's handles.

movements.name: Sledgehammer Strike
movements.description: Swinging a sledgehammer overhead to strike a tire or target, alternating sides — a rotational, explosive conditioning movement.

movements.name: Snatch
movements.description: Barbell pulled from the floor to locked-out overhead in one continuous motion, caught in a full overhead squat — the most technical lift in the sport.

movements.name: Snatch Balance
movements.description: Barbell taken from behind the neck, dropped under with a slight dip and drive, and caught in an overhead squat — a speed-under-the-bar and overhead-stability drill for the snatch.

movements.name: Snatch Pull
movements.description: The pulling portion of a snatch (floor to full extension, wide grip) performed without the catch — builds pull speed and positions without receiving the bar.

movements.name: Sots Press
movements.description: Overhead press performed from the bottom of an overhead squat position, bar taken from behind the neck — an advanced overhead-mobility and stability drill.

movements.name: Split Jerk
movements.description: Jerk received with a front-to-back foot split, one leg forward and one back, locking the bar out overhead — the most common competitive jerk style.

movements.name: Sprint
movements.description: Running a short distance at maximum effort/speed.

movements.name: Squat Clean
movements.description: Barbell pulled from the floor and received in a full-depth front squat, standing up to complete the lift — the standard, most efficient way to move the heaviest load into the front rack.

movements.name: Squat Snatch
movements.description: Snatch received in a full-depth overhead squat, standing to complete the lift — the standard, most efficient snatch technique.

movements.name: Stair Climb
movements.description: Climbing a prescribed number of stairs or flights, typically for time or as part of a larger workout.

movements.name: Stake Drive
movements.description: Driving a stake or post into the ground (or a tire) using an overhead sledgehammer-style strike, a Games-specific strongman-style movement.

movements.name: Stiff Legged Deadlift
movements.description: Deadlift variation with minimal knee bend, hinging almost entirely from the hips — isolates the hamstrings and lower back.

movements.name: Strict Dip
movements.description: Dip performed with no kip or leg swing — a controlled lower and press using triceps/chest/shoulder strength alone.

movements.name: Strict Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up performed with no kip from the legs — a straight, controlled press from the bottom position back to full lockout.

movements.name: Strict Muscle Up
movements.description: Muscle-up performed with no kip or swing — a strict pull straight into the transition and dip lockout.

movements.name: Strict Press
movements.description: Barbell pressed from the front rack to locked-out overhead using shoulder strength alone, with no leg drive or hip dip.

movements.name: Strict Pull Up
movements.description: Pull-up performed with no swing or leg drive — a dead-hang start, pulling the chin over the bar using upper-body strength alone.

movements.name: Sumo Deadlift
movements.description: Deadlift with a wide stance and hands inside the knees, more upright torso than a conventional pull — shifts more of the load to the hips and inner thighs.

movements.name: Sumo Deadlift High Pull
movements.description: Sumo-stance deadlift pulled explosively upward past the hips into a high pull, elbows leading, bar finishing near the collarbone.

movements.name: Swim
movements.description: Swimming a prescribed distance in a pool or open water.

movements.name: Thruster
movements.description: Front squat driven directly into a push press as the athlete stands, using the leg drive out of the bottom of the squat to help send the bar overhead — one continuous squat-to-press movement.

movements.name: Tire Flip
movements.description: Flipping a large tractor tire end over end by squatting to grip its underside and driving it up and over with the hips and legs.

movements.name: Toes To Bar
movements.description: Hanging from a pull-up bar, bringing both feet up to touch the bar between the hands and lowering back to a hang — a kipping or strict core-and-grip movement.

movements.name: Toes To Ring
movements.description: Toes-to-bar performed hanging from gymnastics rings instead of a fixed bar.

movements.name: Triple Under
movements.description: Jump rope pass where the rope passes under the feet three times in a single jump — an advanced, very fast turn.

movements.name: Tuck Jump
movements.description: A vertical jump bringing both knees up toward the chest at the peak before landing.

movements.name: Turkish Get Up
movements.description: A structured, multi-step transition from lying on the floor to standing tall, kettlebell held locked out overhead the entire time — a slow, technical full-body stability movement.

movements.name: V Up
movements.description: Lying flat, simultaneously raising the straight legs and straight torso to meet in a 'V' with hands touching the toes, then lowering back down.

movements.name: Walking Lunge
movements.description: Stepping forward into a lunge, then bringing the back foot through to step into the next lunge, alternating legs while traveling forward.

movements.name: Wall Ball Shot
movements.description: Squatting with a medicine ball held at the chest, then standing and throwing it to hit a target on the wall at a marked height, catching it on the way down into the next squat.

movements.name: Wall Climb
movements.description: Starting in a push-up position with feet against a wall, walking the feet up the wall while walking the hands in until the chest is close to the wall in a handstand — used to reach a handstand position from the ground.

movements.name: Wall Climb Over
movements.description: Wall climb continued until the athlete is fully vertical and then transitions over the top of the wall/obstacle to the other side.

movements.name: Wall Facing Handstand Push Up
movements.description: Handstand push-up performed facing the wall (toes or hands close to it) rather than back to the wall — a stricter balance and lockout standard, often called a chest-to-wall HSPU.

movements.name: Wall Sit
movements.description: Holding a seated squat position with the back against a wall and thighs parallel to the floor, no chair, for time.

movements.name: Weighted Pull Up
movements.description: Pull-up performed with additional external load (a vest, belt, or dumbbell between the feet) beyond bodyweight.

movements.name: Yoke Carry
movements.description: Walking a set distance under a loaded yoke frame resting across the upper back/shoulders — a heavy, stability-demanding loaded carry.
